success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize)
Import-mode adapter was feasible for this codebase (import feasibility 0.9, low intrusiveness risk)
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level validation
Repository structure is mature and modular (potential/orbit/actionAngle/df/util), enabling broad API mapping
No runtime workflow errors or warnings were recorded
failure_reasons: DeepWiki enrichment failed, reducing external contextual analysis depth
Original project test status is marked not passed/executed (execution_time 0, empty details), so upstream compatibility confidence is incomplete
Generated metadata quality is inconsistent (tool_endpoints=0 and LOC/size=0 despite many discovered endpoints/files), indicating observability/reporting defects

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; repository import was reliable but likely slower/less metadata-rich than native clone.
node_performance.analysis_time: Successful static and LLM-direct analysis over a large codebase (~220 files) with useful module targeting; DeepWiki sub-step failed.
node_performance.generation_time: Fast and successful for scaffold creation (7 files), but endpoint accounting/metrics output appears inaccurate.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health checks passed; however, no meaningful functional smoke/regression tests were executed against generated endpoints.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB). Add real measurement during analysis, import, and endpoint invocation.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0%). CPU cost for numerical routines is likely significant under real workloads.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low for generated scaffold (7 files), but reported generated size=0 suggests metrics collection bug rather than true zero usage.
